extraction.one_line_summary: JAKOBSEN Fabio won the sprint after Quick-Step relied on breakaway time gaps rather than train control through the 9km straight finish
tactical_patterns: Quick-Step organized early but didn't establish leadout train, relying on positioning rather than power through 9km straight finish drag.
Breakaway riders contested both intermediate sprints hard for bonus seconds despite knowing they'd be caught, unusual for hopeless breaks.
Lotto-Jumbo controlled pace for race leader Groenewegen but failed to deliver him to podium positions in sprint finale.

rider_signals.JAKOBSEN Fabio: positive: Showed maturity communicating with team car throughout wet conditions, then delivered sprint win without organized leadout train
rider_signals.GROENEWEGEN Dylan: negative: Race leader finished outside top three with no bonus seconds despite Lotto-Jumbo controlling peloton all day
rider_signals.ACKERMANN Pascal: neutral: Second place but couldn't capitalize on Quick-Step's minimal leadout investment in 9km finishing straight
